---
title: "Tidyverse_Exercises"
output: html_document
---

## Write an Rmarkdown script that imports the file
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
height.data <- read_csv("height_data_clean.csv")
```

## then use tidyverse functions to retain data only from January 27, 2023.
```{r}
height.data <- read_csv("height_data_clean.csv")

specific.date <- filter(height.data,survey_date == "2023-01-27")
```

## Summarize the data by computing the average height_cm for each pop (this should be done in just one to several lines of code?you don?t want to write separate code for computing each pop).
```{r}
specific.date %>% group_by(pop) %>% summarize(pop.mean=mean(height_cm))
```
